calls.title: Torrens University Demo 1 (Session 1)
calls.summary: First Torrens demo session covering the portfolio hierarchy, user portals, and the service request to work order workflow. Key improvements identified: category-based problem type filtering and mandatory building/floor fields. Data finalization for assets and PDF templates expected in two days.
key_points: Demonstrated portfolio hierarchy and user portals (maintenance, staff, vendor).
Service request to work order workflow walkthrough; attachment handling clarified.
Category-based filtering for problem types to be added to service request form.
Building and floor fields confirmed mandatory; space field optional.
'Force complete' option available for non-portal vendors.
Data finalization for asset parts and PDF-based inspection/induction templates expected within two days.
decisions: Service request form enhanced with category-based problem type filtering.
Attachments from service requests do not auto-transfer to work orders — must be added independently.

action_items.task: Finalise asset parts data and PDF-based templates

open_items: Asset parts and PDF template data finalization pending.

calls.title: Torrens University Demo 1 (Session 2)
calls.summary: Second Torrens demo session deep-diving external vendor workflow (RFQ, NTE, permit approvals) and asset management. NTE cap confirmed at $800 with admin override; vendor permit prerequisite workflow defined. Floor plan integration and custom email templates are pending items.
key_points: In-depth external vendor workflow: direct assignment, RFQ process, permit approvals, NTE management.
NTE values capped at $800; increases require admin approval with reasoning.
Vendors must obtain permit approvals before starting work.
Work order rejection and reassignment scenarios covered.
Asset management naming conventions and hierarchical filtering discussed.
Customisable email templates for vendors without portal access confirmed.
decisions: NTE cap set at $800 with admin-approval workflow for increases.
Vendors require permit approvals as prerequisite before work commencement.
Asset display filtered hierarchically by building, floor, and space.

action_items.task: Configure customisable vendor email templates
Integrate floor plans for location visualisation

open_items: Floor plan integration and vendor email template configuration pending.

calls.title: AlShalfa - Requirement Session 2
calls.summary: AlShalfa requirement session focused on the work order closure and client verification workflow. Client requires per-WO approval which raises scalability concerns; solution proposed is building-based assignment to multiple client entities. KPI formulas need re-verification and Arabic support has limitations.
key_points: Client insists on verifying each work order (PM and CM) before closure — scalability concern raised.
Proposed solution: assign resolved WOs directly to client users by building association; multiple client entities to manage volume.
Arabic language support confirmed with limitations on translating system statuses.
KPI formulas require re-verification for accuracy.
Ministry/school numbers to be displayed on work orders and building details.
Inventory module: direct purchase requests cannot be initiated; existing inventory can be reserved.
decisions: Resolved WOs assigned directly to client users by building — bypasses Shelfa engineer review step.
Multiple client entities to be created, each linked to specific building groups.
Bulk approval of up to 50 work orders by clients to be implemented.

action_items.task: Configure multiple client entities linked to building groups
Re-verify KPI formulas for accuracy
Add ministry/school numbers to WO and building detail fields

risks: KPI formula accuracy unverified; scalability of client verification process flagged.
open_items: KPI formula re-verification; client entity configuration; inventory module clarification.

key_points: Daily workshop session for CIT CAFM implementation — issue resolution focus.
Sathya attended alongside Ananth, Dinesh, and Magesh alongside full CIT team.
Detailed transcript/summary not available for this session.
decisions: Ongoing issues to be tracked and resolved through daily workshop cadence.

calls.title: GECO - Markup Calculation & Quote PDF
calls.summary: GECO quotation workflow session: markup logic corrected to line-item specific calculation. Outstanding issues on labor duration and tax calculations remain with the Product team. Phase 2 is near completion; check-in/check-out feature expected this week.
key_points: Markup calculation corrected from average-based to line-item specific for accurate client pricing.
PDF builder reviewed: selective display of markup tables and categories confirmed.
Duration and tax calculation discrepancies flagged — pending with Product team.
Work order status display needs internal vs. client-facing view differentiation.
Technicians should see only selling prices (not cost prices) on mobile.
Phase 2 development nearing completion; testing and procurement tasks ongoing.
Check-in/check-out feature for technicians on mobile expected this week.
decisions: Markup calculation changed to line-item specific (not average).
PDF builder customisation options confirmed for quotation display.

action_items.task: Follow up with Product team on duration and tax calculation fix
Differentiate internal vs. client-facing WO status display
Schedule Phase 2 progress review meeting

risks: Duration and tax calculation discrepancies unresolved; pending Product team.
open_items: Product team fix for duration and tax calculations; Phase 2 testing completion.

calls.title: CAFM PDA's Quick Alignment Discussion (ENEC)
calls.summary: ENEC PDA deployment alignment: Facilio CAFM is live at Baraka but PDAs cannot be enabled until vendors are onboarded into ENEC's Active Directory for MDM compliance — a process requiring IT confirmation that may take several weeks. Interim web portal workaround agreed for now.
key_points: Facilio CAFM fully deployed at Baraka — PDA enablement blocked by cybersecurity compliance.
MDM compliance required for controlled device access and data handling.
MDM needs vendors integrated into ENEC Active Directory — IT confirmation pending; may take several weeks.
Interim solution: web portal on mobile devices (not mobile-optimised).
Zone restrictions (Green Zone, PA area): camera and SIM restrictions mainly from OT, not cybersecurity.
Vendor onboarding and device security process needs formal definition and IT approval.
decisions: MDM compliance is the preferred path for PDA deployment.
Web portal on mobile is the interim workaround while MDM process progresses.

action_items.task: Await IT confirmation on Active Directory vendor onboarding timeline
Confirm MDM and AD vendor onboarding process and timeline

risks: MDM implementation dependency on ENEC IT; interim web portal not field-optimised; zone-specific OT restrictions.
open_items: ENEC IT confirmation on Active Directory and MDM vendor onboarding.

calls.summary: Teyseer implementation data session: job plans corrected and imported for Gipco/Gst sites. June 4 go-live on track; historical WO data (Apr 30) targeted for UAT with May data upload by go-live. PM routes and asset association are critical path items not yet finalised.
key_points: Corrected job plans for Gipco and Gst sites imported; Roderick's final comments pending.
Maximo data gaps addressed — missing tasks to be edited/added post-upload.
Inspection template to be updated with hard services examples (HVAC, pumps).
PM routes and asset association identified as critical path for PM execution.
Existing Maximo PM plans to be implemented in Facilio using 'next date' field for PPM triggers.
Historical WO data target: up to Apr 30 for UAT; May data for June 4 go-live.
Module configuration nearly complete; overall implementation on track.
User data upload deferred to UAT phase.

User data upload deferred to UAT to allow self-setup.

calls.title: Facilio <> Berkeley FM — AI & Technician Location Tracking
calls.summary: Berkeley feature scoping session for AI-driven technician tracking. Phase 1 (live tracking, geofencing, out-of-area alerts) is on an accelerated timeline driven by upcoming tenders. Phase 2 introduces AI dispatching. Weekly check-ins established to monitor delivery.
key_points: Phase 1: technician check-in/check-out with live tracking and geofencing — alerts for out-of-area check-ins and disabled GPS.
Phase 2: AI-driven dispatching based on proximity, availability, and performance metrics.
Strict user rights required — only dispatchers, division heads, and project managers can access location data.
Dashboard for technician activity and geofencing data required.
Initial tracking update interval: 5 minutes (potential to reduce).
Accelerated development timeline due to upcoming tenders.
Weekly check-ins scheduled to monitor progress.
decisions: Phase 1 scoped: geofencing + live tracking with alert triggers.
5-minute location update interval agreed as starting point.
Weekly check-in cadence established for progress monitoring.

action_items.task: Deliver Phase 1 location tracking and geofencing feature (accelerated)
Set up weekly check-in cadence with Berkeley

risks: Accelerated timeline pressure; tender deadline creates execution risk if development slips.
open_items: Dev team delivery of Phase 1 geofencing and live tracking; tender timeline.

calls.title: MS Teams — PPM Reporting Discussion
calls.summary: PPM reporting discussion: users expressed frustration with the inability to generate comprehensive PPM reports for non-technical stakeholders. Bulk export limitations and reporting complexity are key pain points. A separate AI features session is to be scheduled.
key_points: Users struggle to generate comprehensive PPM reports — especially for non-technical stakeholders.
Bulk export limitations and reporting complexity are major pain points.
Need for integrated reports consolidating before/after photos, checklists, and recommendations into a single PDF/dashboard.
Tenant portal reporting accessibility needs improvement.
Current process is time-consuming and reliant on technical users.
Separate AI features meeting to be scheduled.
decisions: Separate meeting to be scheduled to explore AI integration and features.

action_items.task: Schedule AI features discussion meeting
Raise PPM reporting bulk export limitations with product/dev

risks: PPM reporting gap may impact tenant satisfaction and system adoption.
open_items: Product roadmap for enhanced PPM reporting and bulk export; AI session scheduling.

Migration from Satie to Gallagher: specific certificates needed to test connection.

calls.title: Ace Facilio Reporting and Troubleshooting
calls.summary: Productive ACE Hardware reporting and troubleshooting session: dashboard creation, PM upload workflows, and bulk asset import covered. Recurring calls established. One risk noted: bulk WO close can inadvertently clear asset operational status, impacting reporting.
key_points: Custom dashboard cards created for asset counts and filtered operational statuses.
PM upload complexity covered: asset-based vs. site-based PMs, manual job plan creation.
Bulk asset import via Excel: field mapping, error handling, and updating via Record ID.
Bulk close of work orders can inadvertently clear 'asset operational status' field — impacting reporting.
Workaround for bulk PM scheduling: create site-level routes and associate multiple assets.
Recurring calls established for system changes and feedback.
decisions: Recurring call cadence established for ongoing feedback and system changes.
Bulk WO close impact on asset status field to be documented as known issue.

action_items.task: Provide regular reports on specified data points for ACE
Document bulk WO close impact on asset operational status

risks: Bulk WO close may clear asset operational status field affecting reporting accuracy.
open_items: Recurring call cadence; bulk close behavior fix or documentation.

calls.title: Weekly Implementation Progress Meeting (SAIC / ARTIC)
calls.crm_account_name: School of the Art Institute of Chicago
calls.summary: SAIC/ARTIC weekly implementation sync: SSO approach simplified to manual bulk uploads; UAT two-phase plan confirmed with Jira tracking. Space data scrolling fix to be demoed Wednesday. Floor plan Markup toolset bug identified for investigation.
key_points: Space data scrolling fix for full names in progress; demo scheduled Wednesday.
SCIM integration too time-consuming — manual bulk uploads adopted for user management.
Custom vanity URL for SSO under exploration; formal quote request to be sent.
Requester portal can bypass login and direct to SSO without vanity URL; maintenance portal requires one.
User list compilation for requester portal underway — target: next Wednesday.
UAT in two phases: Phase 1 with existing data, Phase 2 with new data; Jira for issue tracking.
Admin training for Don and Brad planned.
Floor plan display bug: toolset disappears after clicking 'Markup' — to be investigated.
decisions: Manual bulk upload adopted over SCIM for user management.
UAT two-phase approach confirmed with Jira for tracking.

action_items.task: Demo space data scrolling fix on Wednesday
Send formal quote request for custom SSO vanity URL
Compile user list for requester portal
Investigate floor plan 'Markup' toolset disappearing bug
Schedule admin training for Don and Brad
